name: Current Affairs in Indian Polity (3): Chief Justice of India & Judiciary

sourceRefs.label: scobserver.in/vajiramandravi.com - Justice Surya Kant's appointment as 53rd CJI (Nov 2025); succession list; SC's increased judge strength

researchNotes: Third Polity current-affairs topic (3rd lap). The Supreme Court strength fact directly confirms/extends the Day-63 topic's coverage of the Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026 — that Bill's proposed increase to 37 judges (excluding CJI) is confirmed here as already implemented by mid-2026.

reading.intro: Covers the current Chief Justice of India, the CJI succession process and upcoming appointments, and the Supreme Court's current composition.

qa.question: Who is the current Chief Justice of India, and when was he sworn in?
qa.answer: Justice Surya Kant — the 53rd CJI, sworn in on 24 November 2025.

qa.question: Who was the previous CJI, and why did he demit office?
qa.answer: Justice B.R. Gavai — demitted office on 23 November 2025, upon attaining the mandatory retirement age of 65.

qa.question: How is the Chief Justice of India typically appointed, in practice?
qa.answer: Through the convention of SENIORITY — the outgoing CJI recommends their successor, who is generally the next senior-most judge of the Supreme Court.

qa.question: Who is set to become CJI after Justice Surya Kant, and for what period?
qa.answer: Justice Vikram Nath — from 7 February 2027 to 23 September 2027.

qa.question: What is the Supreme Court's current sanctioned judge strength, and how does this connect to a Bill covered elsewhere?
qa.answer: 37 judges (excluding the CJI) — confirming that the Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026 (already covered in a4-current-affairs-1) had been implemented by mid-2026, increasing strength from the earlier 33.

qa.question: Who are the Supreme Court's current female judges?
qa.answer: Justice B.V. Nagarathna and Justice V. Mohana.
